title: Divisors
description: You are given a sequence of n integers a1, a2, ..., an. You should determine the number of such ordered pairs (i, j), that i, j ∈ {1, ..., n}, i ≠ j and ai is a divisor of aj.
input: The first line of input contains one integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 2,000,000). The second line contains a sequence of n integers a1, a2, ..., an (1 ≤ ai ≤ 2,000,000).
output: In the first and only line of output should contain one integer, denoting the number of pairs sought.
hint: There are 6 pars with the specified properties: (1, 2), (1, 4), (1, 5), (4, 1), (4, 2), (4, 5).
source: ICPC, Regionals, Europe, Central European Regional Contest, Poland Collegiate Programming Contest, AMPPZ 2014, Contest, Open Cup, 2014/2015 Season, Stage 3: Grand Prix of Central Europe
